blurb: Hightouch, a composable customer data platform that plugs directly into Snowflake, BigQuery, and Databricks rather than copying data into a legacy stack, raised a $150M Series D at a $2.75B valuation and now claims $100M in ARR across more than 1,000 customers including Spotify and Warner Music. The pitch has shifted from data activation to agentic marketing, with AI agents that build audiences, generate content variants, and orchestrate across 300-plus channels. At Salomon, the number of people able to create marketing audiences went from a handful to about thirty, which is the real threat to incumbents like Adobe and Salesforce.

title: Exclusive: XCures Lands $46M Series B To Clean Up Messy Medical Records With AI
blurb: XCures raised $46M in a Series B led by Innovius Capital, valuing the medical-records startup at $127M post-money, more than double its December 2023 Series A. The company connects directly to national healthcare interoperability networks and uses AI to structure the industry's "dirty data," the unusable patient records that hospitals have generated for decades. The round lands inside a fast-rising category: investors have put roughly $8.5B into AI-powered health tech this year through June 22, already nearly matching the $8.6B raised across all of 2024.

title: Coval raises $28M as enterprises push voice agents into production
blurb: Voice agents fail in ways ordinary software doesn't, stumbling over accents, background noise, and customers who go off-script, and the manual QA most enterprises still rely on doesn't scale. Coval raised $28M to test and monitor those agents through simulation, the same approach its founder built for Waymo's self-driving stack, and says customers cut QA work up to 30 times and ship 10 times faster. With more than $7B poured into voice AI in Q1 alone, the bottleneck is shifting from building agents to proving they work in production.

title: AI Billionaires Score Win in $29 Million New York Proxy Fight
blurb: AI industry billionaires, including Marc Andreessen and OpenAI president Greg Brockman, won a New York congressional primary that turned into a $29M proxy war over who shapes AI policy. Their super PAC spent more than $8M to defeat Alex Bores, the state assemblyman behind a bill to hold AI companies liable for harms their products cause. The fight signals that frontier-lab money is now moving directly into electoral politics to head off regulation, and founders building in regulated categories should expect the rules to be contested at the ballot box, not just in committee.

title: German startup FINN hits €1B valuation in seven years by building on the belief Europeans would stop buying cars
blurb: FINN, the Munich car-subscription platform, raised a €140M Series D and crossed a €1B valuation seven years after betting that Europeans would shift from owning cars to renting them by the month. The bet is paying off: more than 50,000 active subscriptions and over €300M in ARR, with revenue climbing from €3.2M to €444M between 2022 and 2024. The catch is concentration, as Germany accounts for roughly 34% of a European market projected to grow about 25% a year through 2035, so FINN's home strength is also its main dependency.

title: We're making Bunny DNS free: because a faster internet won't build itself
blurb: Bunny.net eliminated all DNS query fees for Bunny DNS, which now handles nearly 200 billion queries a month across 300,000 domains. The free tier includes DNS hosting for up to 500 domains per account with no query limits or per-request billing, on a routing engine that supports latency-based steering, health checks, and JavaScript logic for dynamic resolution.

title: The OpenAI API everyone copied isn't the one OpenAI recommends
blurb: The author traces why "OpenAI-compatible" tools like Ollama, vLLM, and LM Studio all clone the Chat Completions API rather than the Responses API that OpenAI introduced in March 2025 and now recommends for new projects. The post walks through both formats, explaining how Chat Completions models requests as role-tagged message lists and wraps replies in a choices array, then makes the case that the cloned standard and the recommended one have quietly diverged.

title: GLM 5.2: why I’m replacing Opus in Claude Code with this new model
blurb: Claire Vo ran GLM 5.2, Z.AI's open-weight coding model, through four real tasks in her production Next.js app, including a 45-minute autonomous bug-hunt pulling Sentry and Vercel logs, for $3.36 across roughly 6M tokens. She argues open weights buy cost and vendor independence worth the trade-offs, and reports the model matched ChatPRD's design system on the first try.

title: Testing macOS Notifications in Electron 42 During Local Development
blurb: Electron 42 migrated macOS notifications from the deprecated NSUserNotification API to UNNotification, which requires the app to be code-signed or the Notification object silently emits a failed event instead of showing a banner. This hands-on guide builds a minimal Electron app that logs notification lifecycle events and walks through a free, one-time local code-signing setup so notifications work under npm start without packaging or a paid Apple Developer account.

title: Statistics that live in your SQL
blurb: the-stats-duck shipped v0.6.0, an MIT-licensed DuckDB extension that runs full statistics inside SQL: column profiling via meta(), R-style OLS regression with lm(), bootstrap confidence intervals, and Vega-Lite charts through a VISUALIZE ... DRAW grammar. It runs anywhere DuckDB does, including the browser, so exploratory stats and modeling that used to mean exporting to Python or R now stay in the query.

title: Space4Architecture transforms Brooklyn warehouse into residence
blurb: Space4Architecture converted a 1931 warehouse in Brooklyn's Columbia Street Waterfront District into a 4,400-square-foot residence, retaining the industrial shell behind a black-painted facade and opening the interior with a double-height living space. The project sits on a tight 2,500-square-foot infill parcel, trading the original footprint's bulk for vertical volume while keeping the neighborhood's warehouse vernacular intact.

title: BMW Unveils Striking M Concept Neue Klasse
blurb: BMW revealed the M Concept Neue Klasse, its design study for an electric performance M-series, introducing yellow M Lights, three-dimensional Track Lights, and a trimaran-style front apron that doubles as structural support for the splitter. Industry reports peg it as a near-production design targeting 2027 or 2028, signaling how BMW M intends to carry its visual identity into an EV lineup built on the cleaner Neue Klasse language.

title: Krea 2 Technical Report
blurb: Krea released Krea 2, a series of image foundation models built for creative exploration rather than the single polished default that production diffusion tools tend to converge toward. The system pairs a custom diffusion transformer with a multi-stage pipeline of pretraining, SFT, preference optimization, and RL, plus components like grouped-query attention and a Qwen3-VL text encoder to widen its range across styles, moods, and compositions.

title: Zero-Day Exploitation of Vulnerability (CVE-2026-20245) in Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager
blurb: Mandiant disclosed active zero-day exploitation of CVE-2026-20245, a privilege-escalation flaw in Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager that lets an attacker with a compromised admin account reach root via a malicious CSV upload that bypasses file-upload filtering. The actor, observed at a service provider in early 2026, established rogue SSH peering, altered default account passwords, and ran anti-forensic cleanup scripts to purge indicators, so defenders should patch, rotate appliance credentials, and audit peering configurations.

title: Embedding Forbidden Text in Spyware to Discourage AI Analysis
blurb: A malware developer is prepending JavaScript block comments containing fake nuclear and biological weapons policy text to spyware payloads, aiming to derail LLM-based triage that feeds file headers to a model without isolating them as untrusted data. The trick can trigger refusal, context pollution, or premature classification in naive LLM-first scanners, but it does not evade YARA rules, entropy checks, AST parsing, or behavioral detection.

title: Cisco Unified CM flaw actively exploited to drop webshells (CVE-2026-20230)
blurb: CVE-2026-20230, an unauthenticated SSRF in Cisco Unified Communications Manager, is being actively exploited via Tor to chain into webshell deployment and remote code execution, with honeypots observing automated sweeps after a public PoC dropped this week. The attack abuses WebDialer SSRF to deploy a rogue Apache Axis service and write JSP shells under /platform-services/axis2-web/; apply Cisco's June 3 patches or disable the WebDialer service to mitigate.

title: CISA warns of max severity Ubiquiti flaws exploited in attacks
blurb: CISA added four actively exploited flaws to its KEV catalog, including three max-severity Ubiquiti UniFi OS bugs (CVE-2026-34908, -34909, -34910) that Bishop Fox chained for unauthenticated RCE with elevated privileges, plus a critical Lantronix EDS5000 command-injection (CVE-2025-67038). BOD 26-04 gives federal agencies three days to patch; apply Ubiquiti's May updates and Lantronix firmware 2.2.0.0R1, and run Bishop Fox's detection script to find vulnerable instances.

title: Cordyceps CI/CD Flaws Expose 300+ GitHub Repositories to Supply-Chain Attacks
blurb: Novee Security disclosed Cordyceps, a class of CI/CD misconfiguration in which untrusted pull requests trigger privileged GitHub Actions workflows, enabling command injection, credential theft, and supply-chain compromise exploitable by any unauthenticated user with a free account. A scan of roughly 30,000 high-impact repositories found 300+ fully exploitable, including projects at Microsoft, Google, Apache, and Cloudflare; audit workflows that run on `pull_request_target` and strip write permissions and secrets from untrusted PR triggers.

title: Amadey, StealC malware operations disrupted in Operation Endgame action
blurb: Operation Endgame disrupted the Amadey botnet and StealC infostealer, with Microsoft, Europol, and partners taking down 326 servers and 142 domains tied to the malware-as-a-service operations, alongside the SocGholish loader. The action recovered roughly 27 million credentials stolen from over 385,000 systems; defenders should treat any credentials handled on affected hosts as compromised and force rotation.

title: New ‘Mistic’ RAT Opens Door to Several Ransomware Families
blurb: Symantec and Carbon Black researchers report that the initial access broker tracked as Woodgnat (aka KongTuke), tied to Qilin, Akira, Rhysida, and Black Basta, has been deploying a new RAT, Backdoor.Mistic, since April 2026 via DLL sideloading after ClickFix and Teams helpdesk lures. Defenders should hunt for PowerShell execution from social-engineering chains and DLL sideloading, since Woodgnat profiles compromised hosts to sell ransomware access opportunistically.

title: Exploitable CI/CD Vulnerabilities Expose Millions of Repositories to Hijacking
blurb: Novee disclosed Cordyceps, a systemic class of CI/CD flaws in GitHub Actions YAML that lets unauthenticated attackers with free accounts forge approvals, push code, and exfiltrate cloud credentials, with build tooling from Microsoft, Google, Apache, Cloudflare, and the Python Software Foundation confirmed affected. A single scan flagged 654 repositories and confirmed 300-plus fully exploitable, so audit any low-privilege workflow triggered by untrusted pull requests or comments that feeds output into high-privilege jobs.

title: Critical Ubiquiti Vulnerabilities in Attackers’ Crosshairs
blurb: CISA warns that three critical Ubiquiti UniFi OS flaws (CVE-2026-34908, CVE-2026-34909, and CVE-2026-34910, all CVSS 10) are under active exploitation, chaining an NGINX authentication-gateway bypass into unauthenticated command injection that lets attackers create rogue admin accounts. Ubiquiti patched the bugs in UniFi OS Server 5.0.8 on May 21, but reports indicate zero-day exploitation predating the fix, so operators should patch immediately and audit for unauthorized administrator accounts.

title: macOS Weaknesses Chained to Silently Disable Endpoint Security Agents
blurb: XM Cyber demonstrated a macOS attack chain that lets a standard non-admin user silently unload EDR and MDM agents, abusing weakly validated XPC connections, NIB-file payload injection, and persistence of the kernel code-signing trust cache rather than any kernel exploit. Researchers fully unloaded CrowdStrike Falcon Sensor and permanently deactivated Kandji MDM, which assigned CVE-2026-39118 and patched; an open-source discovery tool, XPC Hunter, is due at Black Hat US in August.

title: Attackers exploit Cisco Unified CM flaw weeks after patch release
blurb: Attackers are actively exploiting CVE-2026-20230 (CVSS 8.6), an SSRF flaw in Cisco Unified CM and Unified CM SME that lets an unauthenticated remote attacker write files to the OS and escalate to root. Cisco patched it on June 3 with no workaround; defenders should patch immediately and, where possible, disable the WebDialer service, which is required for exploitation and off by default.

title: Stealthy Mistic backdoor linked to ransomware access broker KongTuke
blurb: Symantec disclosed Mistic, a stealthy new backdoor active since April in financially motivated intrusions against insurance, education, IT, and professional services organizations, and links it to KongTuke/Woodgnat, an initial access broker that sells footholds to Qilin, Interlock, Rhysida, Akira, 8Base, and Black Basta. Mistic side-loads a malicious version.dll via MpExtMs.exe, runs payloads in memory with no disk artifacts, and includes a self-delete kill switch built for long-term, low-visibility persistence.

title: Microsoft and Allies Smash Shared Infrastructure of Amadey and StealC Malware
blurb: Microsoft, Europol, ESET, Proofpoint, and other partners disrupted the shared command-and-control infrastructure behind the Amadey loader and StealC infostealer under Operation Endgame, targeting hundreds of domains and servers and seizing over 25M stolen credentials from 385,000 systems. Investigators used AI-driven analysis to link the two families' C&C and exploited a flaw in the StealC panel to upload a web shell and collect takedown data.

title: Apple's MacOS Gap Lets Users Disable Security Tools
blurb: Researchers at XM Cyber disclosed a macOS privilege-escalation technique that lets a standard user disable enterprise security tools and invoke root-level functions without administrator credentials, kernel exploits, or triggering alerts. The flaw stems from how macOS caches and reuses an application's CDHash, letting an attacker impersonate trusted components to abuse privileged XPC services, demonstrated against CrowdStrike Falcon EDR and Kandji MDM; Apple has not responded.

title: In a first, a court takedown goes after two cybercrime tools at once
blurb: Microsoft and law enforcement executed a single court-authorized takedown against both Amadey, a malware-delivery botnet, and StealC, a Russia-linked infostealer sold as malware-as-a-service, disrupting over 200 command-and-control servers under the RICO Act. The two tools were tied to more than 140,000 infected machines in the first week of May, so defenders should hunt for Amadey and StealC indicators and rotate any credentials exposed to infostealer activity.

title: White House app auto-downloads to government phones, can't be uninstalled
blurb: The White House automatically pushed its mobile app onto the work phones of millions of federal employees, and workers across the USDA, State Department, and Department of Labor report being unable to uninstall it. The app, identical to the public version, lacks a dedicated privacy or data policy beyond a contact email, raising questions about what device permissions and telemetry it carries on government hardware.

title: London cops bring live facial recognition to West End
blurb: The Metropolitan Police deployed permanent live facial recognition cameras across London's West End, scanning passersby against watchlists in real time. The rollout extends LFR from temporary van-mounted deployments to fixed infrastructure, despite ongoing legal challenges and civil-liberties objections over consent, bias, and the absence of specific statutory authorization in UK law.

title: Medical diagnosis AIs can be tricked into telling whose data trained them
blurb: Researchers showed that medical diagnosis models leak their training data membership, letting an attacker query a model to determine whether a specific patient's records were used to train it. The technique is a membership inference attack against the clinical AI itself, exposing protected health data without breaching the underlying dataset and undermining the de-identification assumptions hospitals rely on when sharing models.

title: Anthropic’s Claude Tag gives AI agents independent identities
blurb: Anthropic introduced an agent identity model for Claude Tag, giving Claude its own administrator-configured identity, permissions, and tool access scoped per workspace or channel rather than borrowing individual users' credentials. The model isolates private channels under separate identities, lets admins revoke all access by disabling one identity instead of auditing per-user ACLs, and shifts the access question from "what can this user do" to "what can this agent do in this compartment.

title: Microsoft's Digital Crimes Unit says AI helped it link two separate hacking tools, Amadey and StealC, and file a single civil lawsuit to help take them down (Lorelei Smillie/Bloomberg)
blurb: Microsoft's Digital Crimes Unit used AI to link the Amadey and StealC malware families and consolidate them into a single civil lawsuit aimed at disrupting both operations. The combined legal action lets Microsoft pursue takedown orders against shared infrastructure in one filing rather than litigating each tool separately, a model the unit signals it will reuse against future malware-as-a-service networks.

title: Microsoft Says Copilot AI Helped Knock Down Cybercrime Tools
blurb: Microsoft said its Copilot AI assisted investigators in dismantling cybercrime tooling, using new AI capabilities to defeat older malware technology. The company is positioning Copilot as an operational asset in takedown operations, though it disclosed few specifics on which families were disrupted or the methods used.

title: NSA Loses Anthropic Mythos Access After June Export-Control Order
blurb: The NSA lost access to Anthropic's Mythos 5 cybersecurity model after the Trump administration's June export-control directive forced the company to suspend its most advanced models for all foreign nationals, including noncitizen employees on US soil. The cutoff hit analysts mid-evaluation, where Mythos had surfaced software vulnerabilities in a controlled red-team test that Sen. Mark Warner initially characterized as breaking into classified systems "in hours.

title: Phishing attack on healthcare firm Xsolis impacts 1.4 million people
blurb: Healthcare technology firm Xsolis confirmed a January 20 phishing attack that gave attackers unauthorized access to its network and exposed files on 1,396,519 individuals, including names, dates of birth, Social Security numbers, health insurance details, and medical treatment information. Xsolis serves over 600 hospitals and insurers, and its disclosure is the third from a healthcare technology vendor in under a month after iRhythm Technologies and Novo Nordisk.

title: EU moves to turn Europol into an operational police force as digital crime climbs
blurb: The European Commission proposed converting Europol from a clearinghouse for national forces into an operational police agency, doubling its staff and earmarking roughly €3bn for it over the next budget period, alongside a technology roadmap on lawful access to encrypted data. Digital-rights groups including European Digital Rights warn the package expands surveillance powers before defining safeguards, with the encryption provisions set to draw the sharpest fight as the proposal moves through Parliament and the Council.

title: Exclusive: Meet AIVEX, a New Triage Model Built to Reduce Supply Chain Threat and Risk
blurb: AIVEX, a CycloneDX VEX extension from researcher Devashri Datta, adds machine-readable exploitation context to vulnerability triage, pairing with a safety relevance interpretation layer (SRIL) that scores how a flaw's impact shifts across AI lifecycle stages. The pair aims to fix SBOM, VEX, and CVSS-based prioritization, which Datta argues misses context and lets supply chain attacks slip through; her SRIL writeup publishes via ISACA on July 1.

title: CISA warns of max severity Ubiquiti flaws exploited in attacks
blurb: CISA added three max-severity Ubiquiti UniFi OS flaws and a critical Lantronix EDS5000 command-injection bug to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog, giving federal agencies three days under directive BOD 26-04 to patch or mitigate. Bishop Fox showed the UniFi flaws can be chained for unauthenticated remote code execution with elevated privileges, so IT teams running these devices should apply the May updates and run the vendor's detection script immediately.

title: Cisco Unified CM flaw actively exploited to drop webshells (CVE-2026-20230)
blurb: Attackers are actively exploiting CVE-2026-20230, an unauthenticated SSRF flaw in Cisco Unified Communications Manager, to drop webshells and gain remote code execution on the underlying server. Cisco patched the bug on June 3 and a public PoC landed this week, so unpatched IP telephony deployments should upgrade now or disable the vulnerable WebDialer service as an interim mitigation.

title: OpenAI and Broadcom unveil LLM-optimized inference chip
blurb: OpenAI and Broadcom introduced Jalapeño, a custom inference chip co-designed to run large language models more efficiently than general-purpose GPUs. The partnership gives OpenAI a path to reduce its dependence on Nvidia for serving capacity, and signals to enterprise buyers that the largest model providers are moving inference onto bespoke silicon to control cost and supply at scale.

title: Amadey, StealC malware operations disrupted in Operation Endgame action
blurb: Microsoft, Europol, and international partners disrupted the Amadey and StealC malware-as-a-service operations under Operation Endgame, seizing 326 servers and 142 domains and recovering roughly 27 million stolen credentials from over 385k compromised systems. The action degrades the initial-access and credential-theft infrastructure that feeds ransomware and fraud campaigns, with Microsoft, ESET, Proofpoint, and IBM X-Force among the private-sector partners supplying intelligence.

title: Zero-Day Exploitation of Vulnerability (CVE-2026-20245) in Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager
blurb: Mandiant disclosed active zero-day exploitation of CVE-2026-20245 in Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager, a privilege-escalation flaw in the file-upload feature that let an attacker turn a compromised admin account into root access via a malicious CSV upload. Enterprises running Catalyst SD-WAN, common across banks, retail, and healthcare branch networks, should prioritize patching and audit for the anti-forensic cleanup Mandiant observed, since attackers reverted configs to erase indicators.

title: Critical Ubiquiti Vulnerabilities in Attackers’ Crosshairs
blurb: CISA warned that threat actors are actively exploiting three critical UniFi OS vulnerabilities (CVE-2026-34908, -34909, and -34910, all rated CVSS 10/10), which chain an NGINX authentication-gateway bypass into unauthenticated command injection on Ubiquiti devices. Ubiquiti patched the flaws in UniFi OS Server 5.0.8 on May 21 without disclosing in-the-wild abuse, but users reported zero-day exploitation that created rogue 'John Sim' administrator accounts on centrally networked infrastructure.

title: In a first, a court takedown goes after two cybercrime tools at once
blurb: Microsoft, working with Europol and partners including ESET, BitSight, IBM X-Force, and Proofpoint, executed a court-authorized takedown that simultaneously disrupted the Amadey botnet and the StealC infostealer, knocking out more than 200 command-and-control servers tied to over 140,000 infected machines. It was the first operation to target two cybercrime tools at once, with the legal team using insights from Copilot to treat both malware families as a single RICO conspiracy.

title: Stealthy Mistic backdoor linked to ransomware access broker KongTuke
blurb: Symantec linked a newly discovered, file-less backdoor called Mistic to KongTuke/Woodgnat, an initial access broker that sells corporate network access to ransomware groups including Qilin, Akira, and Black Basta. Active since April against insurance, education, IT, and professional-services firms, Mistic side-loads through legitimate executables, runs payloads in memory, and includes a self-delete kill switch built for long-term, low-visibility persistence.

title: Boffin claims Microsoft's supposed quantum leap does not compute due to 'basic Python errors'
blurb: Nature published a peer-reviewed critique by University of St Andrews physicist Henry Legg arguing that Microsoft's 2025 Majorana quantum-computing results rest on flawed tune-up software and coding errors that produced incorrect statements to peer reviewers. Legg contends the raw data points to disordered devices incompatible with a topological gap, casting doubt on the Majorana roadmap Microsoft has staked years of research and its Majorana 2 chip announcement on.

title: macOS Weaknesses Chained to Silently Disable Endpoint Security Agents
blurb: XM Cyber demonstrated a macOS attack chain that lets a standard, non-admin user silently unload enterprise endpoint agents, fully disabling CrowdStrike Falcon Sensor and permanently deactivating Kandji MDM without kernel exploits or alerts. The technique abuses legitimate XPC and code-signing trust-cache behavior rather than a software bug, so IT teams relying on macOS EDR and MDM should confirm their vendors have shipped the detections and patches, including Kandji's fix for CVE-2026-39118.

title: Attackers exploit Cisco Unified CM flaw weeks after patch release
blurb: Attackers are now actively exploiting CVE-2026-20230, a critical SSRF flaw in Cisco Unified CM and Unified CM SME, three weeks after Cisco patched it on June 3 and warned it could grant unauthenticated remote attackers root access. The bug carries a CVSS score of 8.6, requires the non-default WebDialer service to be enabled, and has no workaround, leaving patching the only fix for enterprise voice and conferencing deployments.

title: Apple's MacOS Gap Lets Users Disable Security Tools
blurb: Researchers at XM Cyber disclosed a macOS privilege-escalation technique that lets a standard user disable enterprise security tools like CrowdStrike Falcon EDR and Kandji MDM without administrator credentials, kernel exploits, or triggering alerts, by abusing how macOS caches and reuses an application's CDHash to validate trusted XPC services. Any macOS app that exposes privileged XPC services running as root is potentially exposed, and Apple has not yet responded.

title: White House app auto-downloads to government phones, can't be uninstalled
blurb: The White House automatically pushed its official app onto the work phones of millions of federal employees, and IT-managed device controls prevent workers from uninstalling it. Staff at the USDA, State Department, and Department of Labor report the app reappears after deletion and surfaces partisan news feeds alongside a "text President Trump" button, while its App Store listing provides no specific privacy or data policy.

title: London cops bring live facial recognition to West End
blurb: The Metropolitan Police deployed permanent live facial recognition cameras across London's West End, scanning passersby against watchlists in real time. The rollout expands biometric surveillance into one of the city's busiest commercial districts, raising compliance and data-governance questions for enterprises operating in the area amid an unsettled UK regulatory framework.

title: HPE’s Slingshot tops supercomputer interconnects, but China steals the show
blurb: HPE's Slingshot fabric overtook 100G Ethernet as the most common interconnect on the TOP500 for the first time, appearing in 57 systems and four of the top five, including Frontier and Aurora. The headline shift is at the top, where a mysterious Chinese system running an unknown fabric displaced HPE's El Capitan, signaling that domestic interconnect development is advancing despite US export controls on high-end compute.

title: Microsoft and Allies Smash Shared Infrastructure of Amadey and StealC Malware
blurb: Microsoft, Europol, ESET, IBM X-Force, and Proofpoint disrupted the shared command-and-control infrastructure behind the Amadey loader and StealC infostealer under Operation Endgame, seizing 25M stolen credentials from 385,000 systems and flagging $47M in crypto. AI-driven analysis exposed the families' common C&C, letting the coalition target the full cybercrime supply chain rather than individual threats across hundreds of domains and servers.

title: Anthropic’s Claude Tag gives AI agents independent identities
blurb: Anthropic introduced an agent identity model for Claude Tag, giving Claude its own administrator-configured identity, permissions, and tool access scoped per workspace or channel rather than borrowing employee credentials. The model lets IT teams grant Claude repo, data warehouse, or CRM access per channel and revoke everything by disabling one identity, replacing per-user ACLs with compartment-level controls.

title: PayPal Put Agentforce on 8,000 Leads a Month No Human Was Going to Call. Conversions Jumped 50%.
blurb: PayPal deployed a Salesforce Agentforce AI SDR agent into its 200-rep org 14 weeks ago, putting it on the roughly 8,000 monthly merchant leads no human had capacity to chase, and meeting conversion is running about 50% higher than reps working alone. The agent runs a 10-nudge cadence to book qualified meetings and hand reps fully contextualized leads, positioning Agentforce as a funnel-feeder rather than a headcount cut.

title: TSMC is reportedly hiking prices for 'all advanced nodes,' accounting for 74% of the company’s wafer business — Nvidia, AMD, Apple, Qualcomm, and others will face higher wafer costs
blurb: TSMC reportedly told customers to brace for 5–10% price increases across all advanced nodes, extending beyond 3nm and 2nm to 5nm and 7nm and covering 74% of its wafer revenue. Apple, Nvidia, AMD, Qualcomm, Broadcom, and MediaTek now face higher silicon costs that will flow downstream into device and datacenter hardware pricing for enterprise buyers.

title: Microsoft Says Copilot AI Helped Knock Down Cybercrime Tools
blurb: Microsoft said its Copilot security tooling helped investigators dismantle cybercrime infrastructure, using AI to defeat older malware technology in active takedown operations. The disclosure strengthens Microsoft's pitch that Copilot belongs in the enterprise security stack alongside Defender and Sentinel, giving CISOs another reason to consolidate threat-intelligence spend on a single vendor.

title: AI coding agents could soon cost more than the developers using them
blurb: The Register argues that AI coding agents are on track to cost more per month than the developers who run them, as token-metered pricing and agent-orchestrated workflows push consumption far beyond flat per-seat SaaS norms. The shift turns coding tools into a variable, usage-billed line item that IT leaders must budget and cap like cloud compute rather than a predictable per-developer license.

title: Microsoft rivals line up to tell UK watchdog where the software behemoth hurt them
blurb: Microsoft's rivals are submitting evidence to the UK Competition and Markets Authority detailing how the company's licensing and bundling practices have disadvantaged them, escalating the regulator's ongoing probe into Microsoft's cloud and software dominance. For enterprise buyers, any remedies forced on Microsoft could reshape pricing on Azure, Windows Server, and Office licensing terms that currently lock procurement decisions into a single vendor.

title: Phishing attack on healthcare firm Xsolis impacts 1.4 million people
blurb: Healthcare technology firm Xsolis confirmed that a January phishing attack exposed data on 1.4 million people, including Social Security numbers, health insurance details, and medical treatment information across the 600-plus hospitals and insurers it serves. The breach is the third at a healthcare technology vendor in under a month, after iRhythm and Novo Nordisk, putting renewed scrutiny on the security posture of AI software suppliers embedded in provider networks.

title: Nvidia and Samsung to power 6G-ready digital twin for Japan telecom giant
blurb: KDDI launched a joint initiative with Nvidia, Samsung, and Keysight to build a high-fidelity RAN digital twin for training and validating AI-driven network optimization ahead of 6G. Nvidia supplies its Aerial Omniverse Digital Twin and accelerated compute, Samsung contributes vRAN technology, and Keysight provides equipment emulation, with a prototype targeted by March 2028 and platform expansion by 2030.

title: NetApp federates performance-boosted StorageGRIDs into single namespace for greater scale
blurb: NetApp's StorageGRID 12.1 federates object stores into a single namespace scaling to 10EB, with up to 400% higher throughput for AI workloads.

title: Commvault deepens Microsoft tie-up with native Azure resilience service
blurb: Commvault signed a multiyear partnership with Microsoft to offer its data security and cyber recovery tools as a native Azure service, provisionable directly from the platform with no separate infrastructure or manual integration. Azure customers can buy Commvault Cloud through the Microsoft marketplace and count the spend toward their Azure Consumption Commitment, which removes a procurement step and folds recovery tooling into existing prepaid commitments.

title: Minimus container images are now free
blurb: Minimus opened its catalog of hardened, minimal container images to a free tier, with curated Helm charts for deployment. The free images ship without support, SLA, or guaranteed patching timelines, and paid subscriptions may receive security updates first, so teams should weigh the smaller attack surface against slower CVE response before pulling them into production.

title: Exploitable CI/CD Vulnerabilities Expose Millions of Repositories to Hijacking
blurb: Security firm Novee disclosed Cordyceps, a class of CI/CD vulnerabilities in GitHub Actions YAML that lets unauthenticated attackers with a free account forge approvals, push code, and exfiltrate maintainer credentials across build tooling from Microsoft, Google, Apache, Cloudflare, and the Python Software Foundation. A single scan flagged 654 repositories and confirmed over 300 fully exploitable, with the agentic-coding boom reproducing the insecure workflow patterns that traditional scanners skip.

title: Cordyceps CI/CD Flaws Expose 300+ GitHub Repositories to Supply-Chain Attacks
blurb: Novee Security disclosed Cordyceps, a class of CI/CD misconfiguration where untrusted pull requests trigger privileged workflows, and its scan of 30,000 high-impact repos found 300-plus fully exploitable by any unauthenticated user with a free account. Affected projects include Microsoft's Azure Sentinel, Google's adk-samples, Apache Doris, and Cloudflare's Workers SDK, where forged approvals or crafted branch names let attackers run code on CI runners and steal long-lived credentials.

title: We're making Bunny DNS free: because a faster internet won't build itself
blurb: Bunny.net eliminated all DNS query fees for Bunny DNS, removing per-request billing and query limits while bundling free DNS hosting for up to 500 domains per account. The service already routes nearly 200 billion queries monthly across 300,000 domains, offering latency-based routing, health checks, and JavaScript logic that the now-free tier extends without the risk of unpredictable bills from traffic spikes.

title: Walmart buys Vibe.co for $1.4bn to chase Amazon in ads
blurb: Walmart agreed to acquire Vibe.co, a self-serve connected-TV ad platform serving 10,000+ advertisers, for a reported $1.4bn, roughly 12.6 times revenue and more than triple the startup's $410m valuation last September. The multiple signals how aggressively retailers are buying the plumbing behind retail media, folding high-margin advertising into Walmart Connect to close the gap with Amazon.

title: Meta to Unify Creator Marketplace and Partnership Ads Hub, Adds Facebook Creators to Roster
blurb: Meta will merge Creator Marketplace and its Partnership Ads tools into a single Meta Creator Marketing Hub later this year, and is adding Facebook creators alongside the 5M-plus Instagram creators already discoverable in the marketplace. The hub lets brands surface product-tagged and user-generated posts, run pre-permitted content as Partnership Ads, and pull paid performance insights, with Meta citing 19% lower CPA and 13% higher CTR for Partnership Ads over standard campaigns.

title: PayPal Put Agentforce on 8,000 Leads a Month No Human Was Going to Call. Conversions Jumped 50%.
blurb: PayPal deployed an Agentforce AI SDR agent against the roughly 8,000 monthly leads its 200-rep org was never going to work by hand, and meeting conversion is running about 50% higher than reps achieved alone. The agent runs a 10-nudge cadence on every stalled merchant, then hands a qualified, fully contexted lead to a human to close. Salesforce's advice for teams stuck on messy CRM data: don't fix everything first. Stand up a web agent on your site, FAQs, and product data while you consolidate the rest.

title: Avenue Z’s Jonathan Snow: TikTok Shop’s Best Customers Never Show Up in Its P&L
blurb: TikTok Shop's recorded GMV undercounts its real value, argues Avenue Z co-founder Jonathan Snow, because most demand a creator generates converts later on Amazon, a brand's DTC site, or in-store rather than in the app. Snow frames TikTok's roughly 2M commission-only affiliates as an "upper funnel that never existed before," seeding products for free and driving halo sales Sephora has reported in triple digits. The tactical takeaway: judge TikTok Shop on blended channel lift and incrementality, not its standalone P&L.

title: Hightouch : pourquoi cette startup a fait une offre à 1,2 milliard de dollars à Publicis
blurb: Hightouch closed a $150M Series D from Goldman Sachs and Bain Capital Ventures at a $2.75B valuation, building a composable CDP that activates customer data sitting in Snowflake, BigQuery, and Databricks without the legacy baggage of Adobe or Salesforce. The company now claims $100M in ARR and over 1,000 customers including Spotify and Warner Music, and is pushing agentic AI that builds audiences and generates content variants across 300-plus channels.

title: Cboe revives S&P 500 binary options, chasing a market popularized by Polymarket, Kalshi
blurb: Cboe is reviving binary options on the S&P 500, yes-or-no bets on whether the index crosses a set level, more than a decade after pulling similar products that failed to draw interest. The move pushes the exchange directly into prediction-market territory built out by Polymarket and Kalshi, and adds a vertical-spread feature for partial payouts, with Interactive Brokers carrying the contracts and Schwab adding them later this year.

title: The Runes revival: Bitcoin traffic hits a two-year high as transactions blast past 820,000
blurb: Bitcoin processed more than 820,000 transactions in a single day, its highest count since April 2024, as Runes protocol activity drove Runestone transactions above 600,000 and accounted for roughly 25% of all network fees. The surge came with bitcoin trading near $62,000, about 50% below its October high, signaling onchain demand for block space is holding up even through a prolonged downturn.

title: Gold, silver and bitcoin tumble as debasement trade unwinds
blurb: Gold, silver, and bitcoin have all sold off sharply as the 2025 "debasement trade" unwinds, with gold down 28% from its $5,600 January peak, silver off more than 50%, and bitcoin below $62,000 after a 50% correction from its October high. The reversal tracks markets pricing in two 25 basis point Fed hikes by March 2027 under new Chair Kevin Warsh, though bitcoin has gained roughly 30% against gold and 55% against silver since February.

title: The banking lobby is wrong about stablecoins and community banks
blurb: Eco CEO Ryne Saxe argues that the banking lobby's claim that stablecoins will drain deposits from community banks is unproven, and that Congress should not weaken the Digital Asset Market Clarity Act to protect them. He notes community banks hold roughly one-tenth of US banking assets but originate over a third of small business loans and nearly two-thirds of agricultural loans, surviving on relationships rather than settlement speed.

title: Bitcoin OGs aren't selling as aggressively as they did above $100,000
blurb: Long-term bitcoin holders have slashed their selling to the lowest level in nearly two years, with the 90-day average of coins spent by five-year-plus holders dropping to 962 BTC, per CryptoQuant. With bitcoin trading near $63,000, roughly the break-even point for the priciest coins this cohort bought five years ago, the easing OG distribution removes a source of selling pressure that capped prior rallies.

title: Bitcoin’s Retreating Retail Army Exposes Fresh Fragility
blurb: Bitcoin touched a two-week low near $60,900 on Wednesday, down roughly 50% from its October all-time high, as the latest selloff exposes how thin retail demand has become. The institutional flows that brought scale and ETF legitimacy replaced the retail buyers who once absorbed sharp drawdowns, leaving the market more fragile when prices fall.

title: YZi Labs ends proxy war with BNB treasury company CEA Industries
blurb: YZi Labs, the Binance-linked firm formerly known as Binance Labs, ended its activist campaign at BNB treasury company CEA Industries through a cooperation agreement that installs YZi partner Alex Odagiu as interim president and adds Ella Zhang and Matthew Roszak to the board. CEA shares closed 8.35% higher at $2.27 on the news and rose nearly 20% more in Wednesday pre-market trading.

title: NYC-based Taktile, which helps fintechs build automated decision-making workflows, raised a $110M Series C led by Goldman Sachs, and plans a São Paulo office (Camila Grigera Naón/Fortune)
blurb: Taktile, which builds tooling for fintechs to design and run automated decision-making workflows like credit underwriting and fraud checks, raised a $110M Series C led by Goldman Sachs. The round funds a São Paulo office, signaling a push into Latin America, where lenders and neobanks are racing to automate risk decisions that legacy systems handle slowly.

title: Hyperscalers Ditch Canada Investor Calls and Buyers Don’t Care
blurb: Amazon and Alphabet raised C$22.5B ($15.8B) in their first Canadian maple-bond issues to fund AI buildouts, and fund managers absorbed the supply despite the issuers skipping investor calls. The lopsided demand gives US hyperscalers a deep, low-friction funding pool outside the dollar market, and Meta is widely expected to tap it next even though it has signaled no need to.

title: Cboe revives S&P 500 binary options, chasing a market popularized by Polymarket, Kalshi
blurb: Cboe is reviving yes-or-no binary options on the S&P 500 after pulling the products in 2017, entering the prediction-market arena that Polymarket and Kalshi popularized. The contracts, carried by Interactive Brokers and coming to Charles Schwab later this year, add a vertical-spread feature for partial payouts and follow Cboe's same-day S&P 500 options, which now make up roughly 30% of U.S. options volume.

title: The Runes revival: Bitcoin traffic hits a two-year high as transactions blast past 820,000
blurb: Bitcoin processed more than 820,000 transactions in a single day, its highest count since April 2024, with Runes protocol activity driving over 600,000 of them and generating roughly 25% of all network fees. The surge comes with bitcoin trading near $62,000, about 50% below its October high, signaling onchain demand for block space is increasingly tied to token applications rather than simple BTC transfers.

title: Gold, silver and bitcoin tumble as debasement trade unwinds
blurb: Gold has fallen 28% from its January 2025 peak of $5,600 and silver more than 50%, while bitcoin slipped below $62,000, as the 2025 "debasement trade" unwinds on expectations of tighter Fed policy under new chair Kevin Warsh. Markets are now pricing two 25 basis point hikes by March 2027, a reversal of the deficit-and-debt narrative that drove fiat-hedge assets through last year.

title: Waymo Charging Partner Terawatt Raises Up to $300 Million in Debt
blurb: Terawatt Infrastructure raised up to $300M in a five-year senior secured credit facility led by RBC Capital Markets, with Sumitomo Mitsui and UBS in the syndicate, to fund acquisition and development of EV charging sites. The structure, a $150M base with a $150M accordion option, shows banks underwriting infrastructure debt against contracted fleet customers like Waymo rather than the venture equity that funded earlier charging buildouts.

title: The banking lobby is wrong about stablecoins and community banks
blurb: Eco CEO Ryne Saxe argues the banking lobby's claim that stablecoins will drain community-bank deposits is unproven, and that Congress should not weaken the Digital Asset Market Clarity Act, which cleared the Senate Banking Committee 15-9, to protect against it. He notes community banks hold roughly a tenth of US banking assets but originate over a third of small-business loans and nearly two-thirds of agricultural loans, surviving on relationships stablecoins do not replace.

title: Fintech Unicorn Is Said to Tap Citi and EFG for Cairo Listing
blurb: MNT-Halan is preparing an IPO of its Egyptian business in Cairo as soon as this year, working with Citigroup and EFG Hermes on the potential listing. The move adds the microfinance and digital-payments unicorn to a growing pipeline of share sales in Egypt, where local exchanges are courting fintech operators looking to monetize before regional markets tighten.

title: Scammers Are Using AI to Create Fake Auto Loan Documents
blurb: Scammers are using generative AI to forge auto loan documents like pay stubs and income verification, adding fraud losses for US lenders already facing the highest delinquency rates in years. The trend pressures lenders to upgrade KYC and document-verification systems, since AI-fabricated paperwork can pass manual underwriting checks that were built for an era of harder-to-fake records.
